Looking at the current state:
- id=5, color=G, pixels=12, center=[9, 49], cc=O - Dark gray diamond with orange center (top)
- id=23, color=O, pixels=20, center=[25, 48], cc=M - Orange circle with magenta center
- id=29, color=G, pixels=12, center=[35, 45], cc=p - Dark gray diamond with purple center
- id=34, color=G, pixels=12, center=[41, 49], cc=O - Dark gray diamond with orange center
- id=35, color=p, pixels=20, center=[42, 42], cc=M - Purple square with magenta center
- id=40, color=W, pixels=12, center=[49, 40], cc=p - White diamond with purple center (in orange diamond outline)
So the chain objects are:
1. Dark gray diamond [9, 49] with orange center
2. Orange circle [25, 48] with magenta center
3. Dark gray diamond [35, 45] with purple center
4. Dark gray diamond [41, 49] with orange center
5. Purple square [42, 42] with magenta center
6. White diamond [49, 40] with purple center (in orange diamond outline)
Wait, the purple square is at [42, 42], not [41, 49]. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the image:
- The white diamond is in the orange diamond outline at the bottom
- The purple square is below the dark gray diamond [35, 45]
- The dark gray diamond [41, 49] is to the right of the purple square
So the chain is: dark gray [9, 49] → orange circle [25, 48] → dark gray [35, 45] → purple square [42, 42] → dark gray [41, 49] → white diamond [49, 40] (in orange diamond outline).
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me look at the image more carefully.
Looking at the image:
- Top: dark gray diamond with orange center
- Below: orange circle
- Below: dark gray diamond with purple center
- Below: purple square with magenta center
- Right of purple square: dark gray diamond with orange center
- Bottom: white diamond in orange diamond outline
So the chain is: dark gray [9, 49] → orange circle [25, 48] → dark gray [35, 45] → purple square [42, 42] → white diamond [49, 40]. The dark gray diamond [41, 49] is to the right of the purple square, not part of the chain.
